title: Anesthetic, analgesic and antibiotic protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bn9k1","text":"This protocol was used to manage dogs with cruciate ligament disease treated by TPLO","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: acepromazine (Calmivet; Vetoquinol™, Lure, France), 0.05 mg/kg intramuscularly (IM)
diazepam (Valium; Roche™, Boulogne-Billancourt, France), 0.25 mg/kg intravenously (IV)
morphine (Morphine; Lavoisier™, Paris, France), 0.2 mg/kg IV
propofol (PropoVet; Zoetis™, Malakoff, France) 4 mg/kg IV, to effect
isoflurane (Isoflurin; Axience™, Pantin, France), 1.5-2.5%, in 100% oxygen
cefazolin (Cefazoline; Mylan™, Saint-Priest, France), 22 mg/kg IV 30 minutes before incision, repeated every 90 minutes until skin closure

morphine (Morphine; Lavoisier™, Paris, France), 0.2 mg/kg subcutaneously every 4 hours, until discharge cefalexin (Rilexine; Virbac™, Carros, France), 15 mg/kg orally twice daily, for 5 days local cryotherapy every 8 hours, until discharge meloxicam (Metacam; Boehringer Ingelheim™, Reims, France), 0.1 mg/kg orally once daily, for 10 days
creator: geoffrey.pages
